title: Talk:Environmental impact of the Gaza war
wikitext: {{talk header}} {{Contentious topics/talk notice|topic=a-i}} {{old move|date1=31 May 2024|name1=Environmental damage caused by the Israel–Hamas war|destination1=Environmental impact of the Israel–Hamas war|result1=moved|link1=Special:Permalink/1235412026#Requested move 31 May 2024|date2=31 July 2024|destination2=Environmental impact of the Israeli invasion of the Gaza Strip|result2=Other|link2=Special:Permalink/1237829208#Requested move 31 July 2024}} {{DYK talk|29 May|2024|entry=... that Israel's '''[[Environmental impact of the Israel–Hamas war|systematic destruction of trees and farmland in Gaza]]''' has been described as an [[ecocide]]?|nompage=Template:Did you know nominations/Environmental damage of Gaza caused by the Israel–Hamas war}} {{WikiProject banner shell |class=C |1= {{WikiProject Israel|importance=Low}} {{WikiProject Environment|importance=Low}} {{WikiProject Palestine|importance=Low}} {{WikiProject Islam|importance=Low|Islam-and-Controversy=|Sunni=}} {{WikiProject Crime and Criminal Biography|terrorism=yes|terrorism-imp=Low|importance=Low}} {{WikiProject International relations|importance=Low}} {{WikiProject Military history|Asian=|Middle-Eastern=yes|Post-Cold-War=yes|b1=yes|b2=no|b3=yes|b4=yes|b5=no|SciTech=yes}} {{WikiProject Israel Palestine Collaboration}} }} {{User:ClueBot III/ArchiveThis | age =2160 | archiveprefix =Talk:Environmental impact of the Gaza war/Archive | numberstart =1 | maxarchsize =75000 | header ={{Archive}} | minkeepthreads =5 | format = %%i }} ==White Phosphorus== The paragraph about WP cites 2 sources - the first one does reference any environmental damage, the 2nd one does not even mention Gaza. Combining these two sources to make a claim which neither source makes - that the use of WP on Gaza has created an environment impact is textbook [[WP:SYNTH]] (Not to mention that the edit restored by [[User:Richard Nevell]] includes the sentence "This chemical weapon.'." which is explictly contradicted by the very sources uses, '''both''' of which say "WP is '''NOT''' a chemical weapon" [[User:Kentucky Rain24|Kentucky Rain24]] ([[User talk:Kentucky Rain24|talk]]) 22:24, 5 July 2024 (UTC) :This [https://www.reading.ac.uk/news/2024/Expert-Comment/The-environmental-threat-to-Gaza source] links white phosphorus to environmental damage. As for the description of white phosphorus as a chemical weapon, I suggest changing the wording from {{xt|This chemical weapon causes an environmental catastrophe at the level of the basic elements of the environment}} to {{xt|'''It''' causes an environmental catastrophe at the level of the basic elements of the environment}}. [[User:Richard Nevell|Richard Nevell]] ([[User talk:Richard Nevell|talk]]) 23:19, 5 July 2024 (UTC) ::That was not the source in the article, but if you want to add. it, go ahead, attributing it to a claim by the law professor who claimed this, and using similar terminology to what he used , i,e "can have a long term impact" or "could be devastating", rather than stating this as fact. ::If you want to claim 'an environmental catastrophe' - you need to find a source that says that, and hopefully something that explains what "at the level of the basic elements of the environment" means. [[User:Kentucky Rain24|Kentucky Rain24]] ([[User talk:Kentucky Rain24|talk]]) 23:36, 5 July 2024 (UTC) == Does famine belong? == The famine section has some issues, e.g. {{tq|the Gaza Strip is experiencing famine}} is attributed to two opinion pieces, with no attribution. But taking a step back, is there even a clear connection established by reliable sources? The article says famine is due to {{tq|destruction of agricultural land}}, but I don't see that in the current sources. — [[User:XDanielx|xDanielx]] [[User talk:XDanielx|T]]/[[Special:Contributions/XDanielx|C]]\[[Wikipedia:Editor review/xDanielx|R]] 04:51, 19 July 2024 (UTC) :The [[Food and Agriculture Organization]] has definitely drawn a connection between food access and the destruction of agricultural land. See this [[France24]] article from earlier this month: {{tq|Across Gaza, 57 percent of agricultural land has been damaged since the war began, according to a joint assessment published in June by the UN's agriculture and satellite imagery agencies, FAO and UNOSAT. The damage threatens Gaza's food sovereignty, Matieu Henry of the Food and Agriculture Organization told AFP, because 30 percent of the Palestinian territory's food consumption comes from agricultural land.}} [https://www.france24.com/en/live-news/20240707-bulldozed-and-shelled-gaza-s-farming-sector-ravaged-by-war] [[User:CarmenEsparzaAmoux|CarmenEsparzaAmoux]] ([[User talk:CarmenEsparzaAmoux|talk]]) 14:00, 20 July 2024 (UTC) == What should the scope of this article be? == In the [[#Requested move 31 May 2024]] there was some discussion on what the scope of this article. From what I can see, we have three options: *1. One article only – and no other articles – that covers the environmental impact across all theaters of the [[Israel-Hamas war]], including Gaza Strip, Israel, Lebanon, Red Sea etc. *2. One article that gives the overview of the environmental impact of each theater of the war. Theaters with a lot of coverage (e.g Gaza Strip) would break into their own subarticles, while leaving a summary behind in the main article. This would be in accordance with [[Wikipedia:Summary style]]. *3. No overview article. Every theater's environmental impact is discussed in a separate article, with all the other theaters linked through the "See also" section. Currently, it seems this article is #3. There is only content relating to Gaza Strip, and no other theaters. That's not necessarily a bad thing, but the title implies the scope of this article is either #1 or #2. One reason for asking is to determine where should we cover things like [https://apnews.com/article/red-sea-environment-cargo-ship-sinking-houthis-1e130e15ca0863ab40966ea9676cf42b oil spill caused by Houthi attacks], [https://www.barrons.com/news/oil-leaks-toxic-emissions-as-israel-strike-worsens-yemen-pollution-ngos-0f75df30 oil spill caused by IDF attacks], [https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/lebanese-farmers-dig-answers-israels-white-phosphorus-use-2024-07-03/ effect of white phosphorus on Lebanese agriculture], [https://www.jpost.com/environment-and-climate-change/article-783475 release of toxic material in Israel on October 7, 2023]. What do others think should be the scope of this article?'''[[User talk:Vice regent|VR]]''' (Please [[Template:Ping|ping]] on reply) 23:26, 4 August 2024 (UTC) :This article seems the best home for the other information you mention. That would treat this as 1, although 1 and 2 are the same at the moment. This article is only 2200 words, there is no need to create a more concise [[WP:Summary style]], although one may emerge as time goes on. [[User:Chipmunkdavis|CMD]] ([[User talk:Chipmunkdavis|talk]]) 02:09, 5 August 2024 (UTC) ::{{u|Vice regent}} thanks very much for starting this conversation :) Currently I feel like the title is a bit misleading, the content of the article is about environmental damage caused in Gaza by the war but the article title is much broader than that. My personal preference would be to make the article title more specific. [[User:John Cummings|John Cummings]] ([[User talk:John Cummings|talk]]) 15:20, 14 August 2024 (UTC) :::@[[User:John Cummings|John Cummings]], @[[User:Chipmunkdavis|Chipmunkdavis]], I would prefer #3, as I don't see much of a connection between the environmental impact in the various theatres. And as per John, the article is currently about only Gaza but the title appears to be more holistic. Let's see what others say.'''[[User talk:Vice regent|VR]]''' (Please [[Template:Ping|ping]] on reply) 04:28, 19 August 2024 (UTC) ::::{{u|Vice regent}} this sounds good to me, what do you think the title should be? Something like 'Environmental impact of the Israel–Hamas war in Gaza'? That would fit the naming conventions of other articles I think. [[User:John Cummings|John Cummings]] ([[User talk:John Cummings|talk]]) 09:54, 19 August 2024 (UTC) :::::I had suggested "Environmental impact of the Israeli invasion of the Gaza Strip" (because parent article is [[Israeli invasion of the Gaza Strip]]). The exact name can be settled via an RM after the scope is clarified.'''[[User talk:Vice regent|VR]]''' (Please [[Template:Ping|ping]] on reply) 11:19, 20 August 2024 (UTC) ::::::None of the other Environmental impact of X war articles we have divide by theatre ([[Environmental impact of the Vietnam War|Vietnam War]], [[Environmental impacts of war in Afghanistan|war in Afghanistan]], [[Environmental impact of the Gulf wars|Gulf wars]], [[Environmental impact of the Russian invasion of Ukraine|Russian invasion of Ukraine]]). Of course, these are mostly even shorter than this article (only the Russian invasion of Ukraine article is longer, at just under double this one), but this article isn't very long either. [[Chemical weapons in World War I]] is perhaps a more developed similar article, which also does not split by theatre. One of the connections there is the same one as here, it is part of the same war. Keeping potential information together allows readers to have all the context in one place. [[User:Chipmunkdavis|CMD]] ([[User talk:Chipmunkdavis|talk]]) 11:43, 20 August 2024 (UTC) :::::::Hi {{u|Chipmunkdavis}} I think this has happened because the real life situation is different to the other examples of Wikipedia articles on a similar theme. I think its more important for the article title title to be accurate and reflect reality than conform to a naming convention. There might be a rule for this but I'm not sure where I would find it. [[User:John Cummings|John Cummings]] ([[User talk:John Cummings|talk]]) 08:40, 23 August 2024 (UTC) ::::::::All wars have their differences, that doesn't say anything about this particular topic. The current article title is purely descriptive and already reflects reality if that is the concern. [[User:Chipmunkdavis|CMD]] ([[User talk:Chipmunkdavis|talk]]) 09:01, 23 August 2024 (UTC) :I'd be fine with moving this to a title that restricts the scope to Gaza, while still allowing a broader parent article to be made (and/or articles about other theaters), if someone wants to make it. [[User:Levivich|Levivich]] ([[User talk:Levivich|talk]]) 14:33, 19 August 2024 (UTC) ::I am also in favour of that approach. [[User:Richard Nevell|Richard Nevell]] ([[User talk:Richard Nevell|talk]]) 18:12, 20 August 2024 (UTC) :::Hi {{u|Richard Nevell}} what would be the next step in doing this? Should we just have a discussion under a new heading with a couple of options? [[User:John Cummings|John Cummings]] ([[User talk:John Cummings|talk]]) 08:44, 23 August 2024 (UTC) ::::I also favor this option. If it were to be implemented (only pending consensus), would we: ::::*Keep this article as the broader parent article, and summarize the Gaza theatre, and add other theatres. Then create a new [[WP:SPINOFF]] article focused on Gaza and copy this article's content there. Or, ::::*Move this article to be focused on Gaza, while creating a new parent article, with other theatres there. ::::I'd rather do the former.'''[[User talk:Vice regent|VR]]''' (Please [[Template:Ping|ping]] on reply) 15:19, 21 September 2024 (UTC) == Number is incorrectly cited == Under the Pollution>Greenhouse gases section, the last paragraph incorrectly states that: An April 2025 study published in the Social Science Research Network estimated the war would have a long term cost of 31 metric tonnes of carbon dioxide equivalent. The number from the cited article is “31m” which if you follow the citation of that article is correctly interpreted to be 31 million metric tonnes of CO2 equivalent. this is a crucial distinction. [[Special:Contributions/192.80.175.248|192.80.175.248]] ([[User talk:192.80.175.248|talk]]) 23:12, 14 July 2025 (UTC) :Having checked against the source of is clear the 'million' was missing and I have added it in. [[User:Richard Nevell|Richard Nevell]] ([[User talk:Richard Nevell|talk]]) 23:44, 14 July 2025 (UTC) == 53 out of cited 60 references were retracted == this is a highly controversial page based on 53 retracted articles [[User:Akmurb|Akmurb]] ([[User talk:Akmurb|talk]]) 17:42, 9 December 2025 (UTC) :the above comment was incorrect. please, ignore it [[User:Akmurb|Akmurb]] ([[User talk:Akmurb|talk]]) 22:01, 9 December 2025 (UTC)

sections.line: What should the scope of this article be?
53 out of cited 60 references were retracted
